metadata.packName: Power BI Risk Dashboard Definitions for Banking Finance E5 Scenarios
metadata.purpose: Define executive and analyst dashboard pages for visualizing synthetic Microsoft 365 E5, Purview, Defender, DLP, Copilot, endpoint, and insider-risk-style telemetry.

importantModelingNotes: Keep event-level granularity for investigation pages.
Use scenario and correlation IDs to connect multi-workload events.
Use a closed-week or completed-day concept to avoid partial-period drop-offs in trend visuals.
Separate raw event counts from incident or scenario counts.
Treat all synthetic risk scores as demo scoring, not production risk scoring.

dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Provide a concise view of overall synthetic banking data exposure, risky activity, AI usage, and control posture.
primaryQuestions: What is the current risk posture?
Which areas generate the most sensitive data exposure?
Are AI and external sharing risks increasing?
Which controls require attention?

recommendedVisuals.title: Total Risk Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of risky events in selected period

recommendedVisuals.title: High / Critical Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of events with severity High or Critical

recommendedVisuals.title: External Sharing Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of external email, file share, and guest access events

recommendedVisuals.title: AI Risk Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of Copilot and external AI interactions with sensitive content

recommendedVisuals.title: Risk Events Over Time
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Daily or weekly risky event count by severity

recommendedVisuals.title: Risk by Department
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Risk event count by department and severity

recommendedVisuals.title: Risk Category Mix
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: DLP, Labels, AI, Endpoint, Identity, External Sharing

recommendedVisuals.title: Top Active Scenarios
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Scenario count, high severity events, primary user, last activity

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Start with risk posture, then show that the highest risk does not come from one technology alone. It emerges from combinations of oversharing, weak permissions, AI summarization, endpoint movement, and external collaboration.

dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Show where sensitive banking, HR, legal, and customer data appears and how it moves across Microsoft 365 workloads.
primaryQuestions: Which files generate the most sensitive activity?
Which labels are associated with risky events?
Where is regulated financial data being accessed or shared?
Which sensitive files are overexposed?

recommendedVisuals.title: Sensitive Events by Label and Workload
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Risk events grouped by sensitivity label and workload

recommendedVisuals.title: Top Sensitive Files by Risky Event Count
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of risky operations per file

recommendedVisuals.title: Sensitive Data by Department
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Sensitive file events by department

recommendedVisuals.title: Sensitive Data Movement Trend
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Downloads, shares, prints, and copies over time

recommendedVisuals.title: Files Requiring Review
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Files with risky events, weak labels, or external exposure

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Use this page to reinforce the message that discovery and classification are prerequisites for AI readiness and data protection.

dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Operationalize DLP policy matches, warnings, blocks, overrides, and repeat behavior.
primaryQuestions: Which DLP policies are firing?
Who is overriding warnings?
Which files or patterns trigger the most alerts?
Are blocks and warnings aligned with business workflows?

recommendedVisuals.title: DLP Events by Action
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Warned, blocked, override, policy match

recommendedVisuals.title: Top DLP Users
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: DLP event count by user

recommendedVisuals.title: Top DLP Policies
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Events by policy name

recommendedVisuals.title: DLP Overrides Requiring Review
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Override justification, user, file, recipient, label

recommendedVisuals.title: DLP Events Trend
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: DLP events over time by severity

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Focus on DLP as coaching and control, not only blocking. Show where users need clearer workflows or safer alternatives.

dashboardPages.targetAudience: AI Governance, Security, Compliance, Executive Leadership
dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Visualize safe and risky AI usage, including Copilot interactions, sensitive source references, and unmanaged AI app behavior.
primaryQuestions: Which users are interacting with AI around sensitive content?
Which sensitive files are referenced by Copilot?
Where is external AI usage creating exposure?
Are over-permissioned files being surfaced by AI?

recommendedVisuals.title: Copilot Interactions
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Total CopilotInteraction events

recommendedVisuals.title: External AI Interactions
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: AIAppInteraction and UnmanagedAppUpload events

recommendedVisuals.title: Sensitive Sources Referenced by Copilot
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Files referenced by Copilot and their labels

recommendedVisuals.title: AI Usage Risk by User
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Interaction count vs risk score

recommendedVisuals.title: Unsafe AI Prompt Samples
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Prompt preview, source context, risk note, recommended control

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Explain that AI inherits existing access. The key risk is not only the prompt, but the permissions and source content behind the prompt.

dashboardPages.targetAudience: Security Leadership, Insider Risk Analysts, HR and Legal Stakeholders
dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Provide a privacy-aware view of correlated risky sequences involving downloads, sharing, label changes, endpoint movement, AI usage, and contextual HR signals.
primaryQuestions: Which users have correlated risk sequences?
Which sequences need review?
What context exists before concluding intent?
Which cases require HR or Legal involvement?

recommendedVisuals.title: Active Risk Sequences
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Count of synthetic InsiderRiskSequence events

recommendedVisuals.title: Risk Sequences by User
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Sequence count by user

recommendedVisuals.title: Selected User Timeline
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Chronological events for selected user and scenario

recommendedVisuals.title: Signal Combination Matrix
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Downloads, sharing, AI, endpoint, HR context by user

recommendedVisuals.title: Cases Requiring Review
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Scenario, user, severity, context, recommended outcome

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Avoid framing users as malicious by default. Show how sequences require investigation, context, and appropriate HR/Legal guardrails.

dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Visualize external sharing, guest access, recipient domains, and exposed sensitive files.
primaryQuestions: Which departments share externally the most?
Which external domains receive sensitive content?
Are guests accessing restricted spaces?
Which files should have links revoked?

recommendedVisuals.title: External Sharing by Department and Sensitivity
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Event count by department and label

recommendedVisuals.title: Top External Domains
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: External event count by domain

recommendedVisuals.title: User-to-Domain Sharing Map
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Users connected to external recipient domains

recommendedVisuals.title: Active External Links
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: File, owner, label, recipient, expiration, last access

recommendedVisuals.title: Guest Access Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: GuestUserAdded and ExternalUserAccessed events

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Use this page to show how collaboration risk is often driven by business urgency, not only malicious behavior.

dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Show print, USB, network share, and device-level movement involving sensitive banking files.
primaryQuestions: Which sensitive files moved to endpoints?
Which devices generated risky copy or print activity?
Are endpoint DLP controls working?
Which users need review or coaching?

recommendedVisuals.title: Endpoint Actions by Type
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Print, USB copy, network share copy, download

recommendedVisuals.title: Top Devices by Risk Events
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Endpoint event count by DeviceId

recommendedVisuals.title: Sensitive Endpoint Movement
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: User, file, label, device, operation, timestamp

recommendedVisuals.title: Endpoint DLP Trend
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Endpoint events over time

recommendedVisuals.title: Blocked Endpoint Actions
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Endpoint DLP blocked events

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: Endpoint activity gives the story physicality: data is no longer just in the cloud; it is being printed, copied, or moved.

dashboardPages.targetAudience: Demo Presenters, SOC Analysts, Security Architects
dashboardPages.pagePurpose: Replay synthetic scenarios chronologically with workload, user, file, signal, and risk context.
primaryQuestions: What happened first?
Which signals formed the risk sequence?
Which controls reacted?
What would the investigation story look like?

recommendedVisuals.title: Scenario Event Timeline
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Chronological event sequence by scenario

recommendedVisuals.title: Scenario Selector
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Filter by ScenarioId or timeline

recommendedVisuals.title: Event Details
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Time, actor, workload, operation, file, label, risk level

recommendedVisuals.title: Signal Sequence by Workload
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Workload event sequence over time

recommendedVisuals.title: Narrative Summary
recommendedVisuals.measureConcept: Scenario business narrative and recommended outcome

dashboardPages.storytellingGuidance: This page should be presenter-friendly. It should let the audience follow the story before diving into individual controls.

measureDefinitions.description: Count of all activity events in the selected context.
Count of events where IsRiskEvent is true.
Count of events with severity High or Critical.
Count of external sharing, external email, guest access, and external user access events.
Count of Copilot or external AI events involving sensitive content.
Count of print, USB copy, network share copy, and endpoint DLP events.
DLP override events divided by DLP warning events.
Synthetic weighted score based on signal weights and risk modifiers.
Count of users with one or more risk events.
Distinct files involved in external sharing events.

drillthroughDefinitions.name: User Investigation Drillthrough
drillthroughDefinitions.purpose: Allow analysts to move from aggregate user risk to detailed user activity context.

drillthroughDefinitions.name: Sensitive File Drillthrough
drillthroughDefinitions.purpose: Show complete lifecycle and exposure path for a selected sensitive file.

drillthroughDefinitions.name: Scenario Replay Drillthrough
drillthroughDefinitions.purpose: Support presenter-led storytelling and scenario validation.

codexUsageGuidance: Use this file to generate Power BI report specifications or documentation for manual report creation.
Map pseudoDax measures to the actual model once synthetic telemetry tables are implemented.
Use page definitions to create consistent report tabs across demo iterations.
Use storytellingGuidance as speaker notes or page descriptions.
Use drillthroughDefinitions to design investigation navigation paths.
Do not treat synthetic risk scores as production-ready scoring models.
